sections.paragraph:
& מִן־אָז prop. from that time; hence-(a) adv. from of old, formerly, long since, 2Sa 15:34 Isa 16:13 44:8 45:21 48:3 , Isa 48:5, Isa 48:7.-(b) prep. and conj. from (any) time, from when, since; depuis, dès-lors, feit; const. with an inf. Exo 4:10, מֵאָז דַּבֶּרְךָ “since thou hast spoken;” Jos 14:10 with a subst. Rth 2:7, מֵאָז הַבֹּקֶר “from the time of morning,” i.e. since morning. [“In the same sense as מֵאָז בֹּקֶר, Rth 2:7, there is also used אָז מֵהַבֹּקֶר, 2Sa 2:27, which ought, perhaps, to be transposed; comp. לְמִן, כִּי עַל כֵּן. Some consider אָז to be kindred to the pronoun זֶה, so that it would properly have a demonstrative power; which is not unsuitable.” Append.] Psa 76:8, מֵאָז אַפֶּךָ “from the time of thy anger,” i.e. from when once thy anger is kindled. With a finite verb (for מֵאָז אֲשֶׁר), Exo 5:23, מֵאָז בָּאתִי אֶל פַּ״ “from the time when I came unto Pharaoh.” Gen 39:5.

[“Note. Fuller forms from אָז, are אֲזַי (which see), and Ch. אֱרַיִן; the latter seems to have come by softening the letters from הָרֵין, הֵרֵין here, also there; so that its ending seems to be plural, while in fact it is not so. Compare עֲדַיִן for עַר־הֵן. See for these particles and their etymology, Hupfeld in Zeitsch. f. d. Kunde des Morgenl. ii. p. 434”-Ges. add.]
